title: sarothamnus scoparius subsp. reverchonii
intro: Sarothamnus scoparius subsp. reverchonii, also known as Greek gorse or Scotch broom, is a shrub-like plant in the Fabaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting cardiovascular health. Greek gorse can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
